---
#title: "Carwash Analytics"
#author: "Tshepiso M"
#date: "`r Sys.Date()`"
output: 
  html_document:
    theme: flatly
    toc: true
    toc_float: true
    code_folding: hide
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}

#This chunk runs first and sets up everything
knitr::opts_chunk$set(
  echo = FALSE,      # Don't show code in final report
  warning = FALSE,   # Hide warnings
  message = FALSE    # Hide messages
)
# Load required libraries
library(dplyr)       # Provides the %>% pipe operator
library(knitr)       # Provides the kable function
library(kableExtra)  # Provides kable_styling, column_spec, etc.
```

<!-- ====== BANNER ====== -->

<div style="background: linear-gradient(135deg, #1a5276 0%, #2e86c1 100%); 
            color: white; 
            padding: 30px 40px; 
            border-radius: 12px; 
            margin-bottom: 30px;
            text-align: center;
            box-shadow: 0 4px 15px rgba(0,0,0,0.2);">

  <h1 style="margin: 0; font-size: 42px; font-weight: 700;"> Statistical Analysis Report </h1>
  <p style="margin: 8px 0 0 0; font-size: 18px; opacity: 0.9;">
    Statistics for Operational Excellence
  </p>
  <hr style="width: 100px; border: 1px solid rgba(255,255,255,0.3); margin: 15px auto;">
  <p style="margin: 0; font-size: 14px; opacity: 0.8;">
    Prepared by: Tshepiso M &nbsp;|&nbsp; Date: `r format(Sys.Date(), "%B %d, %Y")`
  </p>
</div>

<!-- ====== EXECUTIVE SUMMARY ====== -->
<div style="background: #f8f9fa; 
            padding: 20px 25px; 
            border-radius: 10px; 
            border-left: 5px solid #2e86c1;
            margin-bottom: 30px;
            font-size: 16px;
            line-height: 1.6;">


This report provides a comprehensive analysis of a carwash operational performance enabling strategic improvements and growth opportunities.

</div>


<!-- ====== Statistics summary ====== -->

<style>

  /* Applied to ALL tabs (sets fixed width and alignment) */
  .nav-pills > li > a {
    width: 240px !important;       /* Adjust width as needed */
    text-align: center !important;  /* Centers text inside fixed box */
    border-radius: 6px;
    font-weight: 600;
  }
</style>

## 1. Customer Statistical Analysis
### {.tabset .tabset-pills}

#### Lifetime Value Summary Table

```{r}

#Read data
setwd('C:/Users/tshep/mobile_carwash/data/statistics/customer_metrics')
ltv_summary=read.csv('ltv_summary.csv',header = TRUE)

ltv_summary %>%
  kable(
    caption = "Customer Lifetime Value  Statistical Summary",
    digits = 2
  ) %>%
  kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("striped", "hover", "condensed")) %>%
  column_spec(1, bold = TRUE)
```

#### Insights

<!-- ====== Mean evaluation ====== -->

<div style="background: #ffffff; border-left: 4px solid #1a5276; padding: 16px 20px; margin-top: 20px; border-radius: 0 8px 8px 0; box-shadow: 0 2px 8px rgba(0,0,0,0.04); border: 1px solid #eef2f6; border-left-width: 4px;">
  <h5 style="color: #1a5276; margin: 0 0 8px 0; font-weight: 600; font-size: 15px;"> Average Lifetime Value</h5>
  <p style="margin: 0; color: #4a5568; font-size: 13.5px; line-height: 1.6;">
    The average customer lifetime value sits at <strong>1,191.02</strong>, exceeding the median of <strong>880.00</strong>.
This tells us that a smaller group of "high value" customers is pulling the average up, while the majority of your customer base generates a lower lifetime value. 

This is further proven by standard deviation value of <strong>1054.11</strong>, which informs us that there's an extreme inequality in customer lifetime values. We can then formulate a hypothesis that our data is right skewed until proven otherwise.

The mean value of <strong>1,191.02</strong> cannot be reliable. We can instead opt for the median to make operational decisions 
  </p>
</div>


<!-- ====== shape distribution ====== -->

<div style="background: #ffffff; border-left: 4px solid #1a5276; padding: 16px 20px; margin-top: 20px; border-radius: 0 8px 8px 0; box-shadow: 0 2px 8px rgba(0,0,0,0.04); border: 1px solid #eef2f6; border-left-width: 4px;">
  <h5 style="color: #1a5276; margin: 0 0 8px 0; font-weight: 600; font-size: 15px;"> Shape of Distribution</h5>
  <p style="margin: 0; color: #4a5568; font-size: 13.5px; line-height: 1.6;">
The skewness value resulted to <strong>1.13</strong> while the kurtosis value sits at <strong>4.04</strong>.

This skewness value is over 2 times our benchmark of <strong>0.5</strong>. The statistic proves our hypothesis that our data is positively skewed.
On the other hand, the kurtosis value of <strong>4.04</strong> shows the presence of a heavy tail on the right which means extreme values do exist.
  </p>
</div>




### {.tabset .tabset-pills}

#### Confidence Intervals

```{r}

#Read data
setwd('C:/Users/tshep/mobile_carwash/data/statistics/customer_metrics')
mean_ltv_ci=read.csv('mean_ltv_ci.csv',header = TRUE)

mean_ltv_ci %>%
  kable(
    caption = "Customer Lifetime Value  confindence interval",
    digits = 2
  ) %>%
  kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("striped", "hover", "condensed")) %>%
  column_spec(1, bold = TRUE)
```
#### Insights
<div style="background: #ffffff; border-left: 4px solid #1a5276; padding: 16px 20px; margin-top: 20px; border-radius: 0 8px 8px 0; box-shadow: 0 2px 8px rgba(0,0,0,0.04); border: 1px solid #eef2f6; border-left-width: 4px;">

 <h5 style="color: #1a5276; margin: 0 0 8px 0; font-weight: 600; font-size: 15px;"> Lifetime Value Confidence Interval</h5>
  <p style="margin: 0; color: #4a5568; font-size: 13.5px; line-height: 1.6;">
    Even the absolute worst-case estimate for the population mean <strong>888.24</strong> is higher than what the typical customer actually spends, <strong>880.00</strong>.

Our typical chosen average lifetime value of <strong>880.00</strong>[median] does not fall within the the calculated confidence interval. This proves that taking "confidence" in the mean is dangerous.

Even at the lowest bound of our confidence interval, we would still be overestimating the lifetime value generated by the majority of customers.

  </p>
</div>
